title: Endpoint Malware Coverage
spl:
index=security tag=malware earliest=-30d
| stats dc(dest) as reporting_hosts
| appendcols [| inputlookup cmdb_servers.csv | stats dc(hostname) as total_managed ]
| eval coverage_pct=round(reporting_hosts/total_managed*100,1)
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` dc(Malware_Attacks.dest) as h from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks
description: Hosts with no malware events in 30d may be offline, mis-deployed agents, or blind spots — compare to CMDB.
value: Hosts with no malware events in 30d may be offline, mis-deployed agents, or blind spots — compare to CMDB.
grandmaExplanation: Hosts with no malware events in 30d may be offline, mis-deployed agents, or blind spots — compare to CMDB — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
implementation: Left join finds gaps; alert on critical servers missing telemetry.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Malware + Endpoint inventory.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `Malware_Attacks.dest` distinct vs. `endpoint:inventory`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Left join finds gaps; alert on critical servers missing telemetry.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=malware earliest=-30d
| stats dc(dest) as reporting_hosts
| appendcols [| inputlookup cmdb_servers.csv | stats dc(hostname) as total_managed ]
| eval coverage_pct=round(reporting_hosts/total_managed*100,1)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Endpoint Malware Coverage** — Hosts with no malware events in 30d may be offline, mis-deployed agents, or blind spots — compare to CMDB.

Documented **Data sources**: `Malware_Attacks.dest` distinct vs. `endpoint:inventory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Malware + Endpoint inventory. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, time bounds, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• Adds columns from a subsearch with `appendcols`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **coverage_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(Malware_Attacks.dest) as h from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Endpoint Malware Coverage** — Hosts with no malware events in 30d may be offline, mis-deployed agents, or blind spots — compare to CMDB.

Documented **Data sources**: `Malware_Attacks.dest` distinct vs. `endpoint:inventory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Malware + Endpoint inventory. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Malware.Malware_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (coverage %), Table (missing hosts).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
